delete.title: Delete account
delete.warn: Warning: your identity and personal data will be irreversibly removed. Please enter your password to confirm.

accountDeletion.title: Delete account
accountDeletion.intro: This page explains how to delete your RookHub account and what happens to your data.
accountDeletion.inApp: Sign in to the app and go to Profile → "Delete account". Confirm with your password. The account is deleted immediately.
accountDeletion.removed1: Your identity and personal data (username, email, display/real name, FIDE/ChessResults ID, Chess.com/Lichess name, Discord link) and login access.
accountDeletion.removed2: Personal content and links: repertoires, tournament subscriptions/favourites, friendships and group memberships.
accountDeletion.kept: Aggregated solving/play statistics (e.g. solved puzzles) are retained in anonymised form that cannot be traced back to you.
accountDeletion.contact: For deletion requests or questions, reach us at

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: RookHub is a chess training platform (puzzles, endless mode, courses, repertoires). This policy explains how personal data is processed under the GDPR.
privacy.controller: The controller for data processing is the operator named in the imprint
privacy.dataIntro: Depending on your use, we process:
privacy.dataAccount: Account data: username, email address, password (stored only as a hash).
privacy.dataProfile: Profile data (optional): display/real name, FIDE ID, ChessResults ID, Chess.com/Lichess username, optional Discord link.
privacy.dataUsage: Usage data: solved puzzles, endless/course progress and statistics, play rating, login timestamps.
privacy.dataTechnical: Technical data: IP address, requested endpoints with timestamps and a pseudonymous visitor identifier (reach measurement) in server logs.
privacy.purposes: Providing the account and features (performance of a contract, Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR) as well as operational security, error analysis and reach measurement via logs (legitimate interest, Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).
privacy.thirdIntro: Data is not sold. Depending on your use, the following services are involved:
privacy.thirdDiscord: Discord: only on voluntary account linking (Discord ID/name) for display in the RookHub Discord bot.
privacy.thirdChessresults: chess-results.com: tournament data is fetched server-side when you set a ChessResults ID or load tournaments.
privacy.thirdChesssites: Chess.com / Lichess: only the usernames you voluntarily enter; no automatic data transfer.
privacy.thirdLogging: Elasticsearch/Kibana: self-hosted logging/monitoring on our infrastructure.
privacy.thirdHosting: Hosting: operated on our own server infrastructure with a TLS reverse proxy.
privacy.storage: We do not use tracking cookies. Browser storage (localStorage) holds e.g. your login token, settings and — if enabled — offline puzzles. These remain on your device.
privacy.retention: Account data is stored until you delete your account. You can delete your account at any time — identity and personal data are removed, anonymised statistics are retained. More:
privacy.rights: You have the right to access, rectification, erasure, restriction, data portability and objection, as well as the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (in Austria: the Data Protection Authority).
privacy.contact: For privacy questions, reach us at

impressum.title: Imprint
impressum.disclaimer: Note: the service provider details are placeholders and must be replaced with the actual data before publication. This page is a draft and does not constitute legal advice.

endless.offlineNoCache: You're offline and no run is cached. Open Endless once while online to save puzzles for offline play.

help.title: Endless Puzzle Mode
help.gameplayP1: You solve chess puzzles of increasing difficulty. You start at a configurable rating and work your way up. The rating rises automatically along the Fasttrack phases (see below).
help.gameplayP2: You have 3 lives. At 0 lives the game is over.
help.movesP1: Each puzzle has an intended solution (a sequence of moves). As long as you play the right moves, you follow that solution line.
help.movesP2: If you play a wrong move, Stockfish (a chess engine) takes over the other side. You then play on freely against Stockfish. Its strength can be set via the Stockfish Depth.
help.movesItem1: Checkmating Stockfish counts as an alternative solution — no life lost!
help.movesItem2: Stockfish checkmates you — you lose a life
help.movesItem3: The game against Stockfish continues endlessly until checkmate or Give Up
help.buttonsShowEval: Show Eval — Shows the Stockfish evaluation (Start vs. Now)
help.buttonsReset: Reset — Resets the puzzle, but costs a life
help.buttonsMouseslip: Mouseslip — Undoes the last wrong move (once per puzzle, free)
help.settingsStartRating: Start Rating — The puzzle rating you start at
help.settingsStockfishDepth: Stockfish Depth — Engine strength (1 = very weak, 24 = maximum strength)
help.settingsThemes: Themes — Optional: only certain tactical themes (e.g. "fork pin")
help.fasttrackP1: The increase adapts to your past performance: it analyses your recent sessions and calculates the rating at which you typically lose lives.
help.fasttrackPhase1: Phase 1 (Puzzles 1–5) — Fast increase up to your 1st threshold
help.fasttrackPhase2: Phase 2 (Puzzles 6–10) — Increase from the 1st to the 2nd threshold
help.fasttrackP2: The threshold values (1st/2nd threshold) are calculated automatically but can be adjusted manually. Click "Auto: …" to reset the value.

config.title: Endless Puzzle Mode
config.subtitle: Progressive difficulty — how far can you go?
config.themesHint: Search and pick themes — a puzzle just needs one of them.
config.thresholdExplain: Ratings where you typically lose lives. Adjust to skip easier puzzles faster or slower.
config.curveHint: Rating curve of the gauntlet (fixed when you start):
config.unfinishedRun: Unfinished run: Lv {{level}} | {{solved}} solved | {{lives}} lives | {{max}} max

install.pageIntro: Install RookHub as an app on your device. Pick the option that matches your system.

apk.title: Android app (APK)
apk.desc: Download the native Android app as an APK file and install it manually.
apk.step1: Tap “Download APK” below — ideally directly on your Android device.
apk.step2: Open the downloaded file. If prompted, allow “Install from unknown sources” for your browser.
apk.step3: Confirm the installation — done. RookHub appears as an app in your launcher.
apk.unavailable: Direct APK installation is only possible on Android — not on your current system. You can still download the file and transfer it to an Android device.

pwa.title: Install as an app (PWA)
pwa.desc: Installs RookHub straight from the browser — on Android, Windows, macOS and Linux. Runs in its own window and offline afterwards.
pwa.installed: RookHub is already installed as an app on this device.
pwa.ios2: Tap the Share icon at the bottom.
pwa.ios3: Choose “Add to Home Screen” and confirm with “Add”.
pwa.unavailable: Installing as an app isn’t available in your current browser/system. Open RookHub in Chrome or Edge to install the app.

help.title: Help & Guide
help.subtitle: Everything you need to know about RookHub – step by step.

p: RookHub is your central chess portal: live tournament data from chess-results.com, daily and endless puzzle training, book courses, the Weekly Post, personal training goals, a local engine analysis, plus profile and friend features – all in one place.

Much of it works without an account (puzzles, analysis, daily puzzle). For personal progress, tournament subscriptions, friends and training goals, sign in. This page explains every area – use the table of contents to jump directly.
You can register with a username and password; an email address is optional. Use »Register« in the top bar to create an account, then sign in with the same credentials.
Tick »Stay signed in« at login to remain logged in for 30 days instead of just one. You can sign out any time via the account menu (person icon) at the top right.
In your profile you set your display name and your links: FIDE ID, ChessResults ID, chess.com and Lichess usernames. These connect RookHub to your external chess accounts.
If you store your ChessResults ID, upcoming tournaments are subscribed for you automatically. Your chess.com and Lichess names are used for the play tracking of your training goals.

Use the player search (by last and first name) to find yourself in ChessResults/FIDE and adopt the right ID directly. Your public profile shows others only a reduced view – without your real name, ChessResults ID or Discord.

If the chess bot is active in your Discord, you can link your RookHub account to Discord. The bot sends you a link with a token; if you are logged in the link is established immediately, otherwise after your next sign-in.
Once linked, you appear in daily-puzzle and Weekly-Post leaderboards with your Discord name, and the bot can show you your training progress. You can unlink it again any time in your profile.
Beyond linking, everyone is welcome in our Discord community — join at https://discord.gg/wczc4BJtMf to chat, ask questions and follow announcements.
Under »Friends« you search for other users and send friend requests. Incoming requests appear on the »Requests« tab where you can accept or decline them.
Only the recipient of a request can confirm it. Existing friendships can be removed at any time.

RookHub pulls tournament data live from chess-results.com. In the tournament list you search tournaments and open details with players, teams, pairings and individual results – pick the round from the selector.
Use »Subscribe« to pin a tournament for quick access. The round monitor automatically detects new rounds and crawls them. You can mark players or teams as favorites to highlight them in the view.
For each tournament you can save personal settings such as highlights and notes.
The standard puzzle mode gives you tactical problems from a large pool. You play the solution right on the board; correct and wrong moves are reported instantly.
Puzzles can be filtered by theme. When logged in, your progress including puzzle Elo is saved – without login you can still practice, but progress stays local on the device.
In endless mode you solve a continuous series of increasingly harder puzzles until you make too many mistakes. You set the starting Elo, themes, fast-track thresholds and Stockfish depth yourself.
Your high score and finished sessions are saved; see the history under »Puzzle history« in the account menu and archive old sessions.
Every day (by UTC) there is a fixed daily puzzle that is the same for everyone – so you can compare directly.

After solving you see the leaderboard of all solvers. A »solver« is anyone who solves the puzzle on the first attempt. If you are linked with Discord, your name also appears in the bot's Discord embed.
Courses are imported chess books you work through puzzle by puzzle. Which books you see depends on your group access; administrators see all of them.
You choose between book order (sequential) and random order. Your progress is saved per book and shared across both modes; you can reset a course's progress.
The »Courses« menu item only appears once at least one book has been granted to you.
The Weekly Post mirrors the weekly chess-bot posts: a game or study with a date that you play through puzzle by puzzle. Reading works without login, playing through requires sign-in.
»Done« means you have played all puzzles (solved or not). Giving up or resetting after a move counts as a failed attempt. A live leaderboard shows all participants.

Set daily goals for puzzle and book training (in minutes) plus a weekly goal for games played (rapid/classical) and full training days. Coaches can provide templates per group; your personal goal takes precedence.
The tracker colors each day by goal completion (none/partial/full) – the daily status comes only from puzzle and book time, while playing is a weekly goal.
RookHub fetches rapid/classical games automatically from Lichess and chess.com (public, no login there). Use »Sync now« to trigger it immediately. Bullet, blitz and correspondence do not count.
Under »Statistics« (account menu) you see your puzzle Elo curve and further analysis of your solved puzzles over time.
Analysis runs entirely locally in your browser via Stockfish (WebAssembly) – no server, even offline. You get several main lines (MultiPV) and evaluations for any position.
Analysis works without login. On first start the engine downloads about 7 MB once; afterwards it is available offline too.
Repertoires are collections of PGN files (opening/middlegame/endgame) you upload and manage. You can add files via drag & drop, download them and fetch the combined PGN (max 10 MB per file).
Via the optional browser extension or userscript, your opening repertoires can be checked right while playing on chess.com and lichess.org (see »Browser extension & userscript«).
RookHub is a Progressive Web App: you can install it as an app and use much of it offline. Puzzles, the endless pool, whole books and the Stockfish engine are pre-cached.
Puzzles solved offline are queued and synced automatically once you are back online. An Android install guide including the APK is in the account menu under »Install app«.
Use the sun/moon icon to switch between light, dark and system theme. The globe icon switches the language – RookHub speaks 25 languages and your choice is saved.

In your profile you can create personal API tokens, e.g. for the chess.com browser extension that shows your repertoires right on the board. The raw token is shown only once at creation – copy it immediately.
Tokens have a scope and an optional expiry date and can be revoked at any time.

RepCheck highlights, on chess.com and lichess.org analysis pages, the move at which your game leaves your stored opening repertoire. The repertoire stays on the server – the extension only sends the current game’s move list and gets the deviation back.

Two variants with identical functionality exist: a browser extension (available from the Chrome Web Store and, as a signed build, for Firefox) and a Tampermonkey userscript that runs in any browser with Tampermonkey/Greasemonkey.

Setup: create a token under »Extension tokens« in your profile (scope „extension"), open the gear panel in the repertoire banner on chess.com or lichess.org, enter your RookHub URL and the token, and click »Connect«.

Installation, auto-update and details: https://github.com/kahalm/repcheck — the extension is on the Chrome Web Store at https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/mhddbldcaancdahlochjanpkkboaccpn and as a signed Firefox build at https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/repcheck/, and the Tampermonkey userscript can be imported directly from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kahalm/repcheck/master/repcheck.user.js.
You reach the imprint and privacy policy via the respective pages. You can delete your account yourself: identity and personal data are anonymized, while the anonymized statistics are kept (GDPR).
Found a bug or have a wish? Via »Feedback / report bug« in the footer you reach the issue tracker on GitHub directly. You can open the quick-start overview any time via the info icon in the bar.

chessable.title: Connect Chessable
chessable.intro: Store your Chessable bearer once — RookHub uses it to fetch your courses through the piratechess API. The token is encrypted at rest and never shown in clear text.
chessable.bearerHint: You can grab the bearer from the browser: Chessable → DevTools → Network → header "authorization: Bearer …"
chessable.helpLink: How do I get my bearer token? (help)
chessable.helpIntro: Get your Chessable bearer token from the browser:
chessable.helpStep1: Open Chessable, then the browser DevTools (F12) → Network tab.
chessable.helpStep2: Reload the Chessable dashboard and find the request "getUserSettings".
chessable.helpStep3: In its request headers, find "authorization" — right-click the value and choose "Copy value" (or manually copy the part after "authorization: Bearer "). That is your token (a JWT, valid about 7 days).
chessable.helpNote: Used only to fetch your own purchased courses for personal backup.
chessable.helpRepcheck: Alternatively, the RepCheck browser extension fetches the token for you:
chessable.bookmarkletIntro: Even simpler than copying by hand: a bookmarklet grabs your token on chessable.com and hands it to RookHub.
chessable.bookmarkletDrag: Drag this button to your bookmarks bar:
chessable.bookmarkletStep1: Drag the button above onto your browser's bookmarks bar (one-time setup).
chessable.bookmarkletStep2: Log in to chessable.com as usual (solve the Cloudflare check if it appears).
chessable.bookmarkletStep3: Click the bookmark — RookHub opens and saves your token automatically.
chessable.bookmarkletNoLogin: No Chessable login found. Please log in at chessable.com first, then click again.
chessable.queuePopup: Course added to the queue — come back later. {{ahead}} course(s) ahead of you.
chessable.disclaimerText: Use at your own risk! I don't know whether it is against any TOS. If you get banned on Chessable, that's not my fault. Use only for personal backups.
chessable.importRepertoireDone: “{{name}}” imported as repertoire ({{count}} lines) — find it under Repertoires.
chessable.importBookDone: “{{name}}” imported as book ({{count}} puzzles) — find it under Courses.
chessable.adminImportsSubtitle: Every user's Chessable imports — current queue and history.
chessable.throughputHint: Fetching runs at roughly 15–20 lines/min (including VPN pauses). A course with 500 lines takes about 30 min, 1000 lines about 1 hour.
chessable.cachedHint: Cached — available almost instantly (no Chessable fetch needed)

messages.title: Messages
messages.emptyUser: No messages yet. The admin team will reach you here.
messages.emptyUserCanWrite: No messages yet. Write to the admin team below — they will get back to you here.

available.courses: {{count}} course(s) use an older processing version and can be updated — e.g. to show the book's per-move comments.
available.repertoires: {{count}} repertoire(s) use an older processing version and can be updated.

needsReimport.courses: {{count}} manually uploaded course(s) can't be updated automatically — re-import the PGN to get the latest processing.
needsReimport.repertoires: {{count}} repertoire(s) need a manual re-import to be updated.

reprocess.updateAllTip: Reprocess items with a stored source and re-download Chessable items (needs internet).
reprocess.updateCachedTip: Only reprocess items with a stored source — no download.
reprocess.doneEmpty: Everything is already up to date.
